summary: Epic first set and marathon Truckin'→The Other One jam
blurb: The show delivers a monster first set with tight playing on Cumberland Blues, Jack Straw and China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider. The second‑set jam suite—Truckin' into multiple passages of The Other One, a soaring Morning Dew and a powerful Sing Me Back Home—runs the clock past four hours. Jerry’s soaring guitar and Keith’s piano lock in perfectly while Bill’s drum solo drives the epic jams.
review: May 26, 1972 at London’s Strand Lyceum marks the finale of the Europe ’72 tour with arguably the most ferocious first set ever captured. The band roared through classics like Cumberland Blues, Jack Straw and a blistering China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, each song landing with crisp precision that only a crystal‑clear soundboard could reveal. The second set is an uninterrupted odyssey: Truckin' launches into three sprawling excursions of The Other One, punctuated by Bill Kreutzmann’s thunderous drum solo, before soaring into a heartfelt Morning Dew and a rousing Sing Me Back Home finale. Jerry Garcia’s tone was “soaring” and “perfectly locked” with Keith Godchaux’s piano, while Pigpen’s organ added extra fire on Sing Me. Phil Lesh’s bass provided a solid, esoteric foundation highlighted by a memorable solo before The Other One, and Bob Weir’s rhythm work and vocal moments kept the momentum rolling. Across all four soundboard recordings reviewers agree this night is a high‑water mark of 1972 – a flawless mix, no major dropouts, and an energy that still feels fresh after nearly six decades.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are marked positive; reviewers consistently praise the epic Truckin'→The Other One marathon, soaring guitar and piano lock‑step, powerful drum solo, and flawless soundboard quality. The repeated accolades across multiple sources warrant a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard with excellent full‑spectrum mix, virtually no dropouts, and complete capture of the entire performance.

key_highlights: Monster first set featuring tight playing on Cumberland Blues, Jack Straw and China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider.
Second‑set marathon Truckin' → The Other One (multiple reprises) → Morning Dew → Sing Me Back Home.
Jerry Garcia’s soaring guitar tone locked perfectly with Keith Godchaux’s piano throughout the jams.
Bill Kreutzmann’s powerful drum solo drives the 35‑minute Other One sections.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is described as soaring and perfect, delivering lead work that drives the long jams, especially on Morning Dew.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a solid, tight foundation with an highlighted bass solo before The Other One; his lines are called esoteric and prominent throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Weir offers strong rhythmic counterpoint, memorable vocal moments on Sing Me Back Home, and fun interplay with Garcia during solos.
band_performance.Brent: Keith Godchaux is at his absolute best – fluid piano, front‑line riffs, and a perfect lock‑step with Jerry; Pigpen’s organ contributions also shine.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann’s drum solo is praised as great, and his percussive drive fuels the extended Other One sections.
